content: Just as human language learners strengthen long-term retention and correct usage errors through deliberate post-writing reflection (a core pedagogical application of reflexion), current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to draw from spaced, scaffolded reflection frameworks developed for second-language acquisition to reduce repeated reasoning errors and improve grounded output. This framing reinterprets the original Reflexion autonomous agent framework as not just a technical AI architecture, but a formalization of the same reflective learning process that human educators have used for decades.
